claim: Evidence dispatch and router implementation comprehensively support boundary audit, evidence scope validation, and blocker generation for missing evidence and project mutations. All three agent roles (explorer, cross-checker, verifier) require path citations for project facts, and boundary violations directly block PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() walks git files and directory tree, computes SHA-256 digests for each file outside allowed roots, and excludes allowed write roots from mutation detection.
evidence.why: Boundary audit requires accurate pre-execution baseline to detect target-project mutations.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots() compares before/after digests to detect added, modified, or deleted files and returns sorted change list.
evidence.why: Change detection identifies boundary violations that must block PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: Runtime calls buildBoundarySnapshot(plan.project_root, [plan.output_dir]) to capture pre-execution baseline.
evidence.why: Pre-execution snapshot establishes mutation detection baseline.

evidence.summary: Runtime captures boundaryAfter snapshot, calls diffBoundarySnapshots(), and builds result.boundary object with project_mutation status and changes array.
evidence.why: Post-execution snapshot and diff complete the boundary audit loop.

evidence.summary: Boundary mutations become BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers with path and change details; external research requests without external evidence become EXTERNAL_RESEARCH_EVIDENCE_REQUIRED blockers.
evidence.why: Blockers directly prevent PRD readiness when boundary violations occur.

evidence.summary: Runtime merges boundaryBlockers and externalResearchBlockers into readiness.blockers[], sets prd_ready=false, and updates status/code to blocked/DEMAND_EVIDENCE_DISPATCH_BLOCKED.
evidence.why: Blockers from boundary audit propagate to PRD readiness state.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() classifies evidence as project/external/user/unknown based on scope field, path/url presence, and source field values.
evidence.why: Scope classification ensures evidence sources are correctly declared.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope() checks for valid scope field; evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator() requires non-URL path for project facts.
evidence.why: Validation ensures project-scoped evidence includes concrete path locators.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() generates PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED when project scope lacks path, or PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE when evidence is non-project only.
evidence.why: Blockers prevent agents from presenting project facts without project-scoped path citations.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness() iterates requiredEvidenceRoles, checks evidenceResultComplete(), and generates EVIDENCE_AGENT_RESULT_REQUIRED blockers for incomplete agents.
evidence.why: PRD readiness remains blocked until all evidence agents complete.

evidence.summary: For complete evidence results, inspectDemandPrdReadiness() calls evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues() and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() to add blockers.
evidence.why: Even complete evidence results generate blockers if scope/path validation fails.

evidence.summary: isNonMissingStatusItem() filters status notes like 'no missing data identified'; sanitizeMissing() removes these to keep only actionable gaps.
evidence.why: Prevents status notes from masking actual missing evidence gaps.

evidence.summary: execution_policy boundary block sets project_mutation='forbidden', allowed_write_roots=[output_dir], agent_instruction='boundary_controlled'.
evidence.why: Execution policy enforces non-editing boundary for evidence agents by default.

evidence.summary: Agent prompt hard rule: 'Boundary: do not modify target project files' and 'Allowed artifact/scratch root: output_dir'.
evidence.why: Hard rules explicitly instruct agents to avoid project mutations.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime() checks executeAgents and allowAgentDispatch flags; returns dry_run or blocked status without explicit authorization.
evidence.why: Requires explicit authorization before executing evidence agents.

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation() constructs Claude CLI with '-p', '--permission-mode bypassPermissions', and '--no-session-persistence' for agent dispatch.
evidence.why: Provider configuration enables full-tool agent execution with bypassed permissions for evidence tasks.

risks: Boundary snapshot git ls-files may miss untracked files if exclude patterns change; directory walk mitigates but may have gaps.
Evidence agents might mutate project files via external tooling if provider tool permissions are misconfigured.
sanitizeMissing() regex patterns might misclassify complex status text as missing when it is actually informational.
Provider adapter uses bypassPermissions mode, which relies on agent compliance with hard rules rather than technical enforcement.

result.notes: All 15 explorer claims independently verified from source files. Boundary audit, scope validation, blocker generation, execution authorization, and provider configuration are comprehensively implemented. No blockers found; minor risks documented but do not affect core claim validity.
